functional_requirements.title: Create Order
functional_requirements.description: User creates an order with user ID and item list. Check user existence, product stock, calculate total price, deduct stock, create order record with status pending.

functional_requirements.title: Pay Order
functional_requirements.description: User pays for an order. Check order exists and status is pending, check user balance, deduct balance, update order status to paid.

functional_requirements.title: Ship Order
functional_requirements.description: Ship an order. Check order exists and status is paid, update status to shipped.

functional_requirements.title: Complete Order
functional_requirements.description: Complete an order. Check order exists and status is shipped, update status to completed.

functional_requirements.title: Cancel Order
functional_requirements.description: Cancel an order. Check order exists and status is pending or paid, restore product stock, refund balance if paid, update status to cancelled.

functional_requirements.title: List Orders
functional_requirements.description: List orders with optional filters by user and status. Display order details including items, total price, status, and creation time.

functional_requirements.title: Get User Orders
functional_requirements.description: Get all orders for a specific user. Calculate total spent and count of orders per status.

functional_requirements.title: List Products
functional_requirements.description: List all products with info and stock. Optionally filter low-stock products.

non_functional_requirements.description: Balance and stock must maintain consistency across operations (deduction and restoration).
Order status transitions must follow the correct flow: pending -> paid -> shipped -> completed, with cancellation allowed from pending or paid.

acceptance_criteria.description: Create order successfully when user exists and stock sufficient.
Create order fails with appropriate error when user does not exist.
Create order fails with appropriate error when stock insufficient.
Pay order successfully when order is pending and balance sufficient.
Pay order fails when order not found or status not pending.
Pay order fails when balance insufficient.
Ship order successfully when order is paid.
Complete order successfully when order is shipped.
Cancel order successfully when order is pending or paid, with stock and balance restoration.
Cancel order fails when order status is shipped or completed.
List orders returns correct orders with filters.
Get user orders returns correct statistics.
List products returns correct product list with optional low-stock filter.

storage.details: Three independent in-memory data sources: users, products, orders.

ui.details: External interface via manage_order function with JSON input/output.

glossary.order: A record of a purchase transaction containing user, items, total price, status, and creation time.
glossary.pending: Initial order status after creation, awaiting payment.
glossary.completed: Final order status after delivery completion.
glossary.cancelled: Order status after cancellation, with possible stock and balance restoration.

input_spec.description: Input is a JSON object passed to the manage_order function. The command field specifies the operation, and order_data contains parameters.

schema.command: string (one of: create_order, pay_order, ship_order, complete_order, cancel_order, list_orders, get_user_orders, list_products)

order_data.items: list of objects with product_id (int) and quantity (int) (optional)

output_spec.description: Output is a JSON object with success flag, message, and optional data.
